footer.about: Social agri-processing enterprise in the DRC. Enriched, local and affordable infant flours for every family.
footer.newsletterText: Get our news and impact delivered straight to your inbox.

builtBy.name: Isaac Akonkwa
builtBy.bio: This website was designed and built with great care balancing performance, elegance and a fluid user experience.

careersPage.title: Careers
careersPage.subtitle: Join a team turning local agriculture into lasting nutritional impact.
careersPage.introText: At Food For Life, every talent contributes to a tangible mission: feeding Congolese families and supporting local farmers. We look for committed, creative and rigorous people.

perks.title: Meaningful mission
perks.text: Your work has a direct impact on the health and economy of communities.

perks.title: Growth & learning
perks.text: Grow within a fast-expanding company with real responsibilities.

perks.title: Caring team
perks.text: A collaborative, inclusive environment focused on excellence.

careersPage.formText: Fill in the form below and attach your CV. We'll get back to you shortly.

legalPage.title: Legal notice
legalPage.subtitle: Legal information about the website and the company.

sections.title: Site publisher
sections.body: This website is published by FOOD FOR LIFE , a social agri-processing enterprise registered in the Democratic Republic of Congo, headquartered in Bukavu, South Kivu.

sections.title: Publication director
sections.body: The publication director is the company's legal representative.

sections.title: Intellectual property
sections.body: All content on this website (text, images, logos, videos) is protected by intellectual property law. Any reproduction without prior authorization is prohibited.

sections.title: Liability
sections.body: Food For Life strives to ensure the accuracy of the information published but cannot be held liable for any errors or omissions.

privacyPage.title: Privacy policy
privacyPage.subtitle: How we collect, use and protect your personal data.

sections.title: Data we collect
sections.body: We collect the data you submit through our contact, partnership, application and newsletter forms (name, email, phone, message).

sections.title: Use of data
sections.body: Your data is used solely to respond to your requests, process your application, or send you our news if you have consented.

sections.title: Retention
sections.body: Your data is kept for as long as needed to process your request, then archived or deleted in accordance with applicable regulations.

sections.title: Your rights
sections.body: You have the right to access, rectify and delete your data. To exercise it, contact us via the Contact page.

sections.title: Cookies
sections.body: This website uses cookies essential to its operation as well as anonymized audience-measurement tools.

home.heroSubtitle: Food For Life turns local harvests into fortified, affordable, high-quality infant flours — fighting malnutrition while supporting Congolese farmers.
home.servicesText: An integrated value chain built for nutrition, inclusion and sustainability.
home.productsText: Fortified flours made from local ingredients to the highest quality standards.
home.partnersTrust: They support us in the fight against malnutrition
home.galleryTitle: Our daily work in the field
home.ctaTitle: Let's build a well-nourished future together
home.ctaText: Partners, distributors, institutions: join our mission for a better-fed DRC.

impactPage.title: Our impact
impactPage.subtitle: Numbers that tell the story of lives transformed, farmers supported and better-nourished communities.
impactPage.introText: Every tonne produced, every household served and every job created reflects our commitment: pushing back malnutrition while building a local, sustainable and inclusive agricultural economy in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

services.title: Our services
services.subtitle: An integrated approach, from agricultural production to finished-product distribution.
services.productionText: Supporting local farmers with inputs, seeds and good agricultural practices.
services.processingText: Transforming harvests into fortified flours in modern, hygienic facilities.
services.qualityText: Nutritional testing and strict standards to ensure safe, nutritious products.
services.trainingText: Building the capacity of women and youth across agricultural value chains.
services.distributionText: A local distribution network making nutrition accessible and affordable.
services.nutritionText: Raising family awareness on healthy infant feeding practices.

about.title: About Food For Life
about.subtitle: A social enterprise born from one belief: healthy food is a right, not a privilege.
about.storyText: Founded in the Democratic Republic of Congo, Food For Life emerged from the realization that child malnutrition remains a major challenge, even as the country is rich in agricultural resources. We locally transform maize, soy and other grains into affordable fortified flours, while creating value for farmers.
about.missionText: Fight malnutrition by making quality, locally produced nutrition accessible.
about.visionText: A DRC where every child grows up healthy, powered by sustainable, inclusive agriculture.
about.valueQualityText: High standards at every stage of the value chain.
about.valueInclusionText: Women and youth at the heart of our economic model.
about.valueSustainabilityText: Production that respects resources and communities.
about.valueImpactText: Measuring and maximizing our social effect on the ground.
about.teamSubtitle: Committed women and men in the service of nutrition.
about.teamEmpty: Our team members will be introduced here very soon.

products.title: Our products
products.subtitle: Fortified flours to support every stage of growth.
products.empty: Our products will be showcased here very soon. Check back shortly!

gallery.subtitle: Step into our daily work: fields, production, events and team.

partners.title: Our partners
partners.subtitle: Together we amplify our impact on nutrition and agriculture in the DRC.
partners.empty: We will showcase here the organizations that support us.
